You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"45558076-0bd2-4f93-aa5f-9abc7de10e07","uuid":"a00b47d2-57d4-483c-8473-bfb036ae41a1","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/ghost/core/core/server/api/endpoints/comment-likes.js","content":"// Endpoint for the admin API to return likers for a comment\n\nconst commentsService = require('../../services/comments');\n\n/** @type {import('@tryghost/api-framework').Controller} */\nconst controller = {\n    docName: 'comment_likes',\n    browse: {\n        options: [\n            'id',\n            'page',\n            'limit'\n        ],\n        validation: {\n            options: {\n                id: {\n                    required: true\n                }\n            }\n        },\n        // Use comment permissions - browsing likes is part of comment moderation\n        permissions: {\n            docName: 'comments'\n        },\n        query(frame) {\n            return commentsService.controller.getCommentLikes(frame);\n        }\n    }\n};\n\nmodule.exports = controller;\n","numLines":32,"startLine":1,"totalLines":32}}}

64→    /** @private */\n    65→    checkCommentAccess(memberModel) {\n    66→        if (this.enabled === 'paid' && memberModel.get('status') === 'free') {\n    67→            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n    68→                message: tpl(messages.cannotCommentOnPost)\n    69→            });\n    70→        }\n    71→    }\n    72→\n    73→    /** @private */\n    74→    checkPostAccess(postModel, memberModel) {\n    75→        const access = this.contentGating.checkPostAccess(postModel.toJSON(), memberModel.toJSON());\n    76→        if (access === this.contentGating.BLOCK_ACCESS) {\n    77→            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n    78→                message: tpl(messages.cannotCommentOnPost)\n    79→            });\n    80→        }\n    81→    }\n    82→\n    83→    /** @private */\n    84→    async sendNewCommentNotifications(comment) {\n    85→        await this.emails.notifyPostAuthors(comment);\n    86→\n    87→        if (comment.get('parent_id')) {\n    88→            await this.emails.notifyParentCommentAuthor(comment, {type: 'parent'});\n    89→        }\n    90→        if (comment.get('in_reply_to_id')) {\n    91→            await this.emails.notifyParentCommentAuthor(comment, {type: 'in_reply_to'});\n    92→        }\n    93→    }\n    94→\n    95→    async likeComment(commentId, member, options = {}) {\n comment_id: comment.id,\n   165→            member_id: reporter.id\n   166→        });\n   167→\n   168→        await this.emails.notifyReport(comment, reporter);\n   169→    }\n   170→\n   171→    /**\n   172→     * @param {any} options\n   173→     */\n   174→    async getComments(options) {\n   175→        this.checkEnabled();\n   176→        const page = await the Comment to get replies from\n   226→     * @param {any} options\n   227→     */\n   228→    async getReplies(id, options) {\n   229→        this.checkEnabled();\n   230→        const page = await Comment to get\n   277→     * @param {any} options\n   278→     */\n   279→    async getCommentByID(id, options) {\n   280→        this.checkEnabled();\n   281→        const model = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id}, options);\n   282→\n   283→        if (!model) {\n   284→            throw new errors.NotFoundError({\n   285→                message: tpl(messages.commentNotFound)\n   286→            });\n   287→        }\n   288→\n   289→        return model;\n   290→    }\n   291→\n   292→    /**\n   293→     * @param {string} post - The ID of the Post to comment on\n   294→ [createdAt] - Optional custom created_at timestamp\n   298→     */\n   299→    async commentOnPost(post, member, comment, options, createdAt) {\n   300→        this.checkEnabled();\n   301→        const memberModel = await this.models.Member.findOne({\n   302→            id: member\n   303→        }, {\n   304→            require: true,\n   305→            ...options,\n   306→            withRelated: ['products']\n   307→        });\n   308→\n   309→        this.checkCommentAccess(memberModel);\n   310→\n   311→        const postModel = await this.models.Post.findOne({\n   312→ null,\n   325→            html: comment,\n   326→            status: 'published'\n   327→        };\n   328→\n   329→        if (createdAt) {\n   330→            commentData.created_at = createdAt;\n   331→        }\n   332→\n   333→        const model = await this.models.Comment.add(commentData, options);\n   334→\n   335→        if (!options.context.internal) {\n   336→            await this.sendNewCommentNotifications(model);\n   337→        }\n   338→\n   339→        DomainEvents.dispatch(MemberCommentEvent.create({\n   340→            memberId: member,\n   341→            postId: post,\n   342→            commentId: model.id\n   343→        }));\n   344→\n   345→        // Instead of returning the model, fetch it again, so we have all the relations properly fetched\n   346→        return await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: model.id}, {...options, require: true});\n   347→    }\n   348→\n   349→    /**\n   350→     * @param {string} parent - The ID of the Comment to reply model, fetch it again, so we have all the relations properly fetched\n   434→        return await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: model.id}, {...options, require: true});\n   435→    }\n   436→\n tpl = require('@tryghost/tpl');\nconst errors = require('@tryghost/errors');\nconst {MemberCommentEvent} = require('../../../shared/events');\nconst DomainEvents = require('@tryghost/domain-events');\nconst {byNQL} = require('../../models/base/plugins/bulk-filters');\n\nconst messages = {\n    commentNotFound: 'Comment could not be found',\n    memberNotFound: 'Unable to find member',\n    likeNotFound: 'Unable to find like',\n    alreadyLiked: 'This comment was liked already',\n    replyToReply: 'Can not reply to a reply',\n    commentsNotEnabled: 'Comments are not enabled for this site.',\n    cannotCommentOnPost: 'You do not have permission to comment on this post.',\n    cannotEditComment: 'You do not have permission to edit comments'\n};\n\nclass CommentsService {\n    constructor({config, logging, models, mailer, settingsCache, settingsHelpers, urlService, urlUtils, contentGating, labs}) {\n        /** @private */\n        this.models = models;\n\n        /** @private */\n        this.settingsCache = settingsCache;\n\n        /** @private */\n        this.contentGating = contentGating;\n\n        const Emails = require('./comments-service-emails');\n        /** @private */\n        this.emails = new Emails({\n            config,\n            logging,\n            models,\n            mailer,\n            settingsCache,\n            settingsHelpers,\n            urlService,\n            urlUtils,\n            labs\n        });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @returns {'off'|'all'|'paid'}\n     */\n    get enabled() {\n        const setting = this.settingsCache.get('comments_enabled');\n        if (setting === 'off' || setting === 'all' || setting === 'paid') {\n            return setting;\n        }\n        return 'off';\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    checkEnabled() {\n        if (this.enabled === 'off') {\n            throw new errors.MethodNotAllowedError({\n                message: tpl(messages.commentsNotEnabled)\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    checkCommentAccess(memberModel) {\n        if (this.enabled === 'paid' && memberModel.get('status') === 'free') {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                message: tpl(messages.cannotCommentOnPost)\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    checkPostAccess(postModel, memberModel) {\n        const access = this.contentGating.checkPostAccess(postModel.toJSON(), memberModel.toJSON());\n        if (access === this.contentGating.BLOCK_ACCESS) {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                message: tpl(messages.cannotCommentOnPost)\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** @private */\n    async sendNewCommentNotifications(comment) {\n        await this.emails.notifyPostAuthors(comment);\n\n        if (comment.get('parent_id')) {\n            await this.emails.notifyParentCommentAuthor(comment, {type: 'parent'});\n        }\n        if (comment.get('in_reply_to_id')) {\n            await this.emails.notifyParentCommentAuthor(comment, {type: 'in_reply_to'});\n        }\n    }\n\n    async likeComment(commentId, member, options = {}) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n\n        const memberModel = await this.models.Member.findOne({\n            id: member.id\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['products']\n        });\n\n        this.checkCommentAccess(memberModel);\n\n        const data = {\n            member_id: memberModel.id,\n            comment_id: commentId\n        };\n\n        const existing = await this.models.CommentLike.findOne(data, options);\n\n        if (existing) {\n            throw new errors.BadRequestError({\n                message: tpl(messages.alreadyLiked)\n            });\n        }\n\n        return await this.models.CommentLike.add(data, options);\n    }\n\n    async unlikeComment(commentId, member, options = {}) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n\n        try {\n            await this.models.CommentLike.destroy({\n                ...options,\n                destroyBy: {\n                    member_id: member.id,\n                    comment_id: commentId\n                },\n                require: true\n            });\n        } catch (err) {\n            if (err instanceof this.models.CommentLike.NotFoundError) {\n                return Promise.reject(new errors.NotFoundError({\n                    message: tpl(messages.likeNotFound)\n                }));\n            }\n\n            throw err;\n        }\n    }\n\n    async reportComment(commentId, reporter) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId}, {require: true});\n\n        // Check if this reporter already reported this comment (then don't send an email)?\n        const existing = await this.models.CommentReport.findOne({\n            comment_id: comment.id,\n            member_id: reporter.id\n        });\n\n        if (existing) {\n            // Ignore silently for now\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Save report model\n        await this.models.CommentReport.add({\n            comment_id: comment.id,\n            member_id: reporter.id\n        });\n\n        await this.emails.notifyReport(comment, reporter);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async getComments(options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const page = await this.models.Comment.findPage({...options, parentId: null});\n\n        return page;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @typedef {Object} AdminBrowseAllOptions\n     * @property {boolean} includeNested - If true, include replies in flat list; if false, only top-level comments\n     * @property {string[]} [withRelated] - Relations to include (e.g. ['member', 'post'])\n     * @property {string} [filter] - NQL filter string\n     * @property {Function} [mongoTransformer] - Function to transform parsed NQL filter\n     * @property {{op: string, value: number}} [reportCount] - Filter by report count (op: '=', '>', '>=', '<', '<=', '!=')\n     * @property {string} order - Order string (e.g. 'created_at desc')\n     * @property {number} [page] - Page number\n     * @property {number} [limit] - Results per page\n     */\n\n    /**\n     * Browse all comments across the site for admin moderation.\n     * Does not check if comments are enabled - admins can moderate existing comments.\n     *\n     * Service responsibility: Business logic and data access.\n     * Receives clean, typed parameters - no frame/HTTP knowledge.\n     *\n     * @param {AdminBrowseAllOptions} options\n     */\n    async getAdminAllComments({includeNested, filter, mongoTransformer, reportCount, order, page, limit}) {\n        return await this.models.Comment.findPage({\n            withRelated: ['member', 'post', 'count.replies', 'count.likes', 'count.reports', 'inReplyTo', 'parent'],\n            filter,\n            mongoTransformer,\n            reportCount,\n            order,\n            page,\n            limit,\n            parentId: includeNested ? undefined : null,\n            isAdmin: true,\n            browseAll: true\n        });\n    }\n\n    async getAdminComments(options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const page = await this.models.Comment.findPage({...options, parentId: null, isAdmin: true});\n\n        return page;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to get replies from\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async getReplies(id, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const page = await this.models.Comment.findPage({...options, parentId: id});\n\n        return page;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get reporters for a comment (admin only)\n     * @param {string} commentId - The ID of the Comment to get reporters for\n     * @param {any} options - Query options (page, limit)\n     */\n    async getCommentReporters(commentId, options = {}) {\n        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId});\n\n        const {page, limit} = options;\n        const result = await this.models.CommentReport.findPage({\n            filter: `comment_id:'${commentId}'`,\n            withRelated: ['member'],\n            order: 'created_at desc',\n            page,\n            limit\n        });\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get likes for a comment (admin only)\n     * @param {string} commentId - The ID of the Comment to get likes for\n     * @param {any} options - Query options (page, limit)\n     */\n    async getCommentLikes(commentId, options = {}) {\n        const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId});\n\n        const {page, limit} = options;\n        const result = await this.models.CommentLike.findPage({\n            filter: `comment_id:'${commentId}'`,\n            withRelated: [],\n            order: 'created_at desc',\n            page,\n            limit\n        });\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to get\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async getCommentByID(id, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id}, options);\n\n        if (!model) {\n            throw new errors.NotFoundError({\n                message: tpl(messages.commentNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        return model;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} post - The ID of the Post to comment on\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to comment as\n     * @param {string} comment - The HTML content of the Comment\n     * @param {any} options\n     * @param {Date} [createdAt] - Optional custom created_at timestamp\n     */\n    async commentOnPost(post, member, comment, options, createdAt) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const memberModel = await this.models.Member.findOne({\n            id: member\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['products']\n        });\n\n        this.checkCommentAccess(memberModel);\n\n        const postModel = await this.models.Post.findOne({\n            id: post\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['tiers']\n        });\n\n        this.checkPostAccess(postModel, memberModel);\n\n        const commentData = {\n            post_id: post,\n            member_id: member,\n            parent_id: null,\n            html: comment,\n            status: 'published'\n        };\n\n        if (createdAt) {\n            commentData.created_at = createdAt;\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.add(commentData, options);\n\n        if (!options.context.internal) {\n            await this.sendNewCommentNotifications(model);\n        }\n\n        DomainEvents.dispatch(MemberCommentEvent.create({\n            memberId: member,\n            postId: post,\n            commentId: model.id\n        }));\n\n        // Instead of returning the model, fetch it again, so we have all the relations properly fetched\n        return await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: model.id}, {...options, require: true});\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} parent - The ID of the Comment to reply to\n     * @param {string} inReplyTo - The ID of the Reply to reply to\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to comment as\n     * @param {string} comment - The HTML content of the Comment\n     * @param {any} options\n     * @param {Date} [createdAt] - Optional custom created_at timestamp\n     */\n    async replyToComment(parent, inReplyTo, member, comment, options, createdAt) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const memberModel = await this.models.Member.findOne({\n            id: member\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['products']\n        });\n\n        this.checkCommentAccess(memberModel);\n\n        const parentComment = await this.getCommentByID(parent, options);\n        if (!parentComment) {\n            throw new errors.BadRequestError({\n                message: tpl(messages.commentNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        if (parentComment.get('parent_id') !== null) {\n            throw new errors.BadRequestError({\n                message: tpl(messages.replyToReply)\n            });\n        }\n\n        const postModel = await this.models.Post.findOne({\n            id: parentComment.get('post_id')\n        }, {\n            require: true,\n            ...options,\n            withRelated: ['tiers']\n        });\n\n        this.checkPostAccess(postModel, memberModel);\n\n        let inReplyToComment;\n        if (parent && inReplyTo) {\n            inReplyToComment = await this.getCommentByID(inReplyTo, options);\n\n            // we only allow references to published comments to avoid leaking\n            // hidden data via the snippet included in API responses\n            if (inReplyToComment && inReplyToComment.get('status') !== 'published') {\n                inReplyToComment = null;\n            }\n\n            // we don't allow in_reply_to references across different parents\n            if (inReplyToComment && inReplyToComment.get('parent_id') !== parent) {\n                inReplyToComment = null;\n            }\n        }\n\n        const commentData = {\n            post_id: parentComment.get('post_id'),\n            member_id: member,\n            parent_id: parentComment.id,\n            in_reply_to_id: inReplyToComment && inReplyToComment.get('id'),\n            html: comment,\n            status: 'published'\n        };\n\n        if (createdAt) {\n            commentData.created_at = createdAt;\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.add(commentData, options);\n\n        if (!options.context.internal) {\n            await this.sendNewCommentNotifications(model);\n        }\n\n        DomainEvents.dispatch(MemberCommentEvent.create({\n            memberId: member,\n            postId: parentComment.get('post_id'),\n            commentId: model.id\n        }));\n\n        // Instead of returning the model, fetch it again, so we have all the relations properly fetched\n        return await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: model.id}, {...options, require: true});\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to delete\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to delete as\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async deleteComment(id, member, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const existingComment = await this.getCommentByID(id, options);\n\n        if (existingComment.get('member_id') !== member) {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                // todo fix message\n                message: tpl(messages.memberNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.edit({\n            status: 'deleted'\n        }, {\n            id,\n            require: true,\n            ...options\n        });\n\n        return model;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {string} id - The ID of the Comment to edit\n     * @param {string} member - The ID of the Member to edit as\n     * @param {string} comment - The new HTML content of the Comment\n     * @param {any} options\n     */\n    async editCommentContent(id, member, comment, options) {\n        this.checkEnabled();\n        const existingComment = await this.getCommentByID(id, options);\n\n        if (!comment) {\n            return existingComment;\n        }\n\n        if (existingComment.get('member_id') !== member) {\n            throw new errors.NoPermissionError({\n                message: tpl(messages.cannotEditComment)\n            });\n        }\n\n        const model = await this.models.Comment.edit({\n            html: comment,\n            edited_at: new Date()\n        }, {\n            id,\n            require: true,\n            ...options\n        });\n\n        return model;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Bulk update comment status based on NQL filter\n     * @param {string} filter - NQL filter string (e.g., \"member_id:'abc123'+status:published\")\n     * @param {string} status - New status ('hidden', 'published', 'deleted')\n     */\n    async bulkUpdateStatus(filter, status) {\n        await this.models.Comment.bulkEditWhere({\n            data: {status},\n            where: byNQL(filter)\n        });\n    }\n\n    async getMemberIdByUUID(uuid, options) {\n        const member = await this.models.Member.findOne({uuid}, options);\n\n        if (!member) {\n            throw new errors.NotFoundError({\n                message: tpl(messages.memberNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n\n        return member.id;\n    }\n}\n\nmodule.exports = CommentsService;\n","numLines":522,"startLine":1,"totalLines":522}}}

require('@tryghost/errors');\n     3→const nql = require('@tryghost/nql');\n     4→const {splitFilter} = require('@tryghost/mongo-utils');\n     5→\n     6→/**\n     7→ * @typedef {import('@tryghost/api-framework').Frame} Frame\n     8→ */\n     9→\n    10→const {MethodNotAllowedError} = require('@tryghost/errors');\n    11→const tpl = require('@tryghost/tpl');\n    12→\n    13→const messages = {\n    14→    cannotDestroyComments: 'You cannot destroy comments.',\n    15→    memberNotFound: 'Unable to find member'\n    16→};\n    17→\n    18→module.exports = class CommentsController {\n    19→    /**\n    20→     * @param {import('./comments-service')} service\n    21→     * @param {import('./comments-stats-service')} stats\n    22→     */\n    23→    constructor(service, stats) {\n    24→        this.service = service;\n    25→        this.stats = stats;\n    26→    }\n    27→\n    28→    async #setImpersonationContext(options) {\n    29→        if (options.impersonate_member_uuid) {\n    30→            options.context = options.context || {};\n    31→            options.context.member = options.context.member || {};\n    32→            options.context.member.id = await this.service.getMemberIdByUUID(options.impersonate_member_uuid);\n    33→        }\n    34→    }\n    35→\n    36→    #checkMember(frame) {\n    37→        if (!frame.options?.context?.member?.id) {\n    38→            throw new errors.UnauthorizedError({\n    39→                message: tpl(messages.memberNotFound)\n    40→            });\n    41→        }\n    42→    }\n    43→\n    44→    /**\n    45→     * Extract count.reports from filter string.\n    46→     * Returns the remaining filter, a reportCount option, and a mongoTransformer.\n    47→     *\n    48→     * @param {string} [filterString]\n    49→     * @returns {{filter: string|undefined, reportCount: {op: string, value: number}|undefined, mongoTransformer: Function|undefined}}\n    50→     */\n    51→    #extractReportCountFilter(filterString) {\n    52→        if (!filterString || !filterString.includes('count.reports')) {\n    53→            return {filter: filterString, reportCount: undefined, mongoTransformer: undefined};\n    54→        }\n    55→\n    56→        const parsed = nql(filterString).parse();\n    57→        const [countReportsFilter, remainingFilter] = splitFilter(parsed, ['count.reports']);\n    58→\n    59→        // Convert count.reports to reportCount option\n    60→        let reportCount;\n    61→        if (countReportsFilter?.['count.reports'] !== undefined) {\n    62→            const val = countReportsFilter['count.reports'];\n    63→            if (typeof val === 'number') {\n    64→                reportCount = {op: '=', value: val};\n    65→            } else if (val.$gt !== undefined) {\n    66→                reportCount = {op: '>', value: val.$gt};\n    67→            } else if (val.$gte !== undefined) {\n    68→                reportCount = {op: '>=', value: val.$gte};\n    69→            } else if (val.$lt !== undefined) {\n    70→                reportCount = {op: '<', value: val.$lt};\n    71→            } else if (val.$lte !== undefined) {\n    72→                reportCount = {op: '<=', value: val.$lte};\n    73→            } else if (val.$ne !== undefined) {\n    74→                reportCount = {op: '!=', value: val.$ne};\n    75→            }\n    76→        }\n    77→\n    78→        // If there's remaining filter, use transformer to replace parsed result\n    79→        // Otherwise, no filter needed\n    80→        if (remainingFilter && Object.keys(remainingFilter).length > 0) {\n    81→            return {\n    82→                filter: filterString, // Keep original string for NQL to parse\n    83→                reportCount,\n    84→                mongoTransformer: () => remainingFilter // Replace with our pre-split result\n    85→            };\n    86→        }\n    87→\n    88→        return {filter: undefined, reportCount, mongoTransformer: undefined};\n    89→    }\n    90→\n    91→    /**\n    92→     * @param {Frame} frame\n    93→     */\n    94→    async browse(frame) {\n    95→        if (frame.options.post_id) {\n    96→            if (frame.options.filter) {\n    97→                frame.options.mongoTransformer = function (query) {\n    98→                    return {\n    99→                        $and: [\n   100→                            {\n   101→                                post_id: frame.options.post_id\n   102→                            },\n   103→                            query\n   104→                        ]\n   105→                    };\n   106→                };\n   107→            } else {\n   108→                frame.options.filter = `post_id:${frame.options.post_id}`;\n   109→            }\n   110→        }\n   111→\n   112→        return await this.service.getComments(frame.options);\n   113→    }\n   114→\n   115→    async adminBrowse(frame) {\n   116→        if (frame.options.post_id) {\n   117→            if (frame.options.filter) {\n   118→                frame.options.mongoTransformer = function (query) {\n   119→                    return {\n   120→                        $and: [\n   121→                            {\n   122→                                post_id: frame.options.post_id\n   123→                            },\n   124→                            query\n   125→                        ]\n   126→                    };\n   127→                };\n   128→            } else {\n   129→                frame.options.filter = `post_id:${frame.options.post_id}`;\n   130→            }\n   131→        }\n   132→\n   133→        frame.options.isAdmin = true;\n   134→        // Admin routes in Comments-UI lack member context due to cross-domain constraints (CORS), which prevents\n   135→        // credentials from being passed. This causes issues like the inability to determine if a\n   136→        // logged-in admin (acting on behalf of a member) has already liked a comment.\n   137→        // To resolve this, we retrieve the `impersonate_member_uuid` from the request params and\n   138→        // explicitly set it in the context options as the acting member's ID.\n   139→        // Note: This approach is applied to several admin routes where member context is required.\n   140→        await this.#setImpersonationContext(frame.options);\n   141→        return await this.service.getAdminComments(frame.options);\n _ = require('lodash');\nconst errors = require('@tryghost/errors');\nconst nql = require('@tryghost/nql');\nconst {splitFilter} = require('@tryghost/mongo-utils');\n\n/**\n * @typedef {import('@tryghost/api-framework').Frame} Frame\n */\n\nconst {MethodNotAllowedError} = require('@tryghost/errors');\nconst tpl = require('@tryghost/tpl');\n\nconst messages = {\n    cannotDestroyComments: 'You cannot destroy comments.',\n    memberNotFound: 'Unable to find member'\n};\n\nmodule.exports = class CommentsController {\n    /**\n     * @param {import('./comments-service')} service\n     * @param {import('./comments-stats-service')} stats\n     */\n    constructor(service, stats) {\n        this.service = service;\n        this.stats = stats;\n    }\n\n    async #setImpersonationContext(options) {\n        if (options.impersonate_member_uuid) {\n            options.context = options.context || {};\n            options.context.member = options.context.member || {};\n            options.context.member.id = await this.service.getMemberIdByUUID(options.impersonate_member_uuid);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #checkMember(frame) {\n        if (!frame.options?.context?.member?.id) {\n            throw new errors.UnauthorizedError({\n                message: tpl(messages.memberNotFound)\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Extract count.reports from filter string.\n     * Returns the remaining filter, a reportCount option, and a mongoTransformer.\n     *\n     * @param {string} [filterString]\n     * @returns {{filter: string|undefined, reportCount: {op: string, value: number}|undefined, mongoTransformer: Function|undefined}}\n     */\n    #extractReportCountFilter(filterString) {\n        if (!filterString || !filterString.includes('count.reports')) {\n            return {filter: filterString, reportCount: undefined, mongoTransformer: undefined};\n        }\n\n        const parsed = nql(filterString).parse();\n        const [countReportsFilter, remainingFilter] = splitFilter(parsed, ['count.reports']);\n\n        // Convert count.reports to reportCount option\n        let reportCount;\n        if (countReportsFilter?.['count.reports'] !== undefined) {\n            const val = countReportsFilter['count.reports'];\n            if (typeof val === 'number') {\n                reportCount = {op: '=', value: val};\n            } else if (val.$gt !== undefined) {\n                reportCount = {op: '>', value: val.$gt};\n            } else if (val.$gte !== undefined) {\n                reportCount = {op: '>=', value: val.$gte};\n            } else if (val.$lt !== undefined) {\n                reportCount = {op: '<', value: val.$lt};\n            } else if (val.$lte !== undefined) {\n                reportCount = {op: '<=', value: val.$lte};\n            } else if (val.$ne !== undefined) {\n                reportCount = {op: '!=', value: val.$ne};\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If there's remaining filter, use transformer to replace parsed result\n        // Otherwise, no filter needed\n        if (remainingFilter && Object.keys(remainingFilter).length > 0) {\n            return {\n                filter: filterString, // Keep original string for NQL to parse\n                reportCount,\n                mongoTransformer: () => remainingFilter // Replace with our pre-split result\n            };\n        }\n\n        return {filter: undefined, reportCount, mongoTransformer: undefined};\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async browse(frame) {\n        if (frame.options.post_id) {\n            if (frame.options.filter) {\n                frame.options.mongoTransformer = function (query) {\n                    return {\n                        $and: [\n                            {\n                                post_id: frame.options.post_id\n                            },\n                            query\n                        ]\n                    };\n                };\n            } else {\n                frame.options.filter = `post_id:${frame.options.post_id}`;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return await this.service.getComments(frame.options);\n    }\n\n    async adminBrowse(frame) {\n        if (frame.options.post_id) {\n            if (frame.options.filter) {\n                frame.options.mongoTransformer = function (query) {\n                    return {\n                        $and: [\n                            {\n                                post_id: frame.options.post_id\n                            },\n                            query\n                        ]\n                    };\n                };\n            } else {\n                frame.options.filter = `post_id:${frame.options.post_id}`;\n            }\n        }\n\n        frame.options.isAdmin = true;\n        // Admin routes in Comments-UI lack member context due to cross-domain constraints (CORS), which prevents\n        // credentials from being passed. This causes issues like the inability to determine if a\n        // logged-in admin (acting on behalf of a member) has already liked a comment.\n        // To resolve this, we retrieve the `impersonate_member_uuid` from the request params and\n        // explicitly set it in the context options as the acting member's ID.\n        // Note: This approach is applied to several admin routes where member context is required.\n        await this.#setImpersonationContext(frame.options);\n        return await this.service.getAdminComments(frame.options);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Browse all comments across the site (admin only, no post_id required)\n     * Used for admin moderation page showing comments from all posts\n     *\n     * Controller responsibility: Parse frame into clean domain parameters.\n     * The frame should not pass beyond this layer.\n     *\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async adminBrowseAll(frame) {\n        // Query params can be strings or booleans depending on how the request is made\n        const includeNestedParam = frame.options.include_nested;\n        const includeNested = includeNestedParam !== 'false' && includeNestedParam !== false;\n\n        // Extract count.reports from filter (it needs special handling as raw SQL)\n        const {filter, reportCount, mongoTransformer} = this.#extractReportCountFilter(frame.options.filter);\n\n        return await this.service.getAdminAllComments({\n            includeNested,\n            filter,\n            mongoTransformer,\n            reportCount,\n            order: frame.options.order || 'created_at desc',\n            page: frame.options.page,\n            limit: frame.options.limit\n        });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async replies(frame) {\n        return this.service.getReplies(frame.options.id, _.omit(frame.options, 'id'));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async adminReplies(frame) {\n        frame.options.isAdmin = true;\n        frame.options.order = 'created_at asc'; // we always want to load replies from oldest to newest\n        await this.#setImpersonationContext(frame.options);\n\n        return this.service.getReplies(frame.options.id, _.omit(frame.options, 'id'));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async read(frame) {\n        await this.#setImpersonationContext(frame.options);\n        return await this.service.getCommentByID(frame.data.id, frame.options);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async edit(frame) {\n        this.#checkMember(frame);\n\n        let result;\n        if (frame.data.comments[0].status === 'deleted') {\n            result = await this.service.deleteComment(\n                frame.options.id,\n                frame?.options?.context?.member?.id,\n                frame.options\n            );\n        } else {\n            result = await this.service.editCommentContent(\n                frame.options.id,\n                frame?.options?.context?.member?.id,\n                frame.data.comments[0].html,\n                frame.options\n            );\n        }\n\n        if (result) {\n            const postId = result.get('post_id');\n            const parentId = result.get('parent_id');\n            const pathsToInvalidate = [\n                postId ? `/api/members/comments/post/${postId}/` : null,\n                parentId ? `/api/members/comments/${parentId}/replies/` : null\n            ].filter(path => path !== null);\n            frame.setHeader('X-Cache-Invalidate', pathsToInvalidate.join(', '));\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async add(frame) {\n        this.#checkMember(frame);\n        const data = frame.data.comments[0];\n\n        let result;\n        if (data.parent_id) {\n            result = await this.service.replyToComment(\n                data.parent_id,\n                data.in_reply_to_id,\n                frame.options.context.member.id,\n                data.html,\n                frame.options\n            );\n        } else {\n            result = await this.service.commentOnPost(\n                data.post_id,\n                frame.options.context.member.id,\n                data.html,\n                frame.options\n            );\n        }\n\n        if (result) {\n            const postId = result.get('post_id');\n            const parentId = result.get('parent_id');\n            const pathsToInvalidate = [\n                postId ? `/api/members/comments/post/${postId}/` : null,\n                parentId ? `/api/members/comments/${parentId}/replies/` : null\n            ].filter(path => path !== null);\n            frame.setHeader('X-Cache-Invalidate', pathsToInvalidate.join(', '));\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    async destroy() {\n        throw new MethodNotAllowedError({\n            message: tpl(messages.cannotDestroyComments)\n        });\n    }\n\n    async count(frame) {\n        if (!frame?.options?.ids) {\n            return await this.stats.getAllCounts();\n        }\n\n        const ids = frame?.options?.ids.split(',');\n\n        return await this.stats.getCountsByPost(ids);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async like(frame) {\n        this.#checkMember(frame);\n\n        const result = await this.service.likeComment(\n            frame.options.id,\n            frame.options?.context?.member,\n            frame.options\n        );\n\n        const comment = await this.service.getCommentByID(frame.options.id);\n\n        if (comment) {\n            const postId = comment.get('post_id');\n            const parentId = comment.get('parent_id');\n            const pathsToInvalidate = [\n                postId ? `/api/members/comments/post/${postId}/` : null,\n                parentId ? `/api/members/comments/${parentId}/replies/` : null\n            ].filter(path => path !== null);\n            frame.setHeader('X-Cache-Invalidate', pathsToInvalidate.join(', '));\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async unlike(frame) {\n        this.#checkMember(frame);\n\n        const result = await this.service.unlikeComment(\n            frame.options.id,\n            frame.options?.context?.member,\n            frame.options\n        );\n\n        const comment = await this.service.getCommentByID(frame.options.id);\n\n        if (comment) {\n            const postId = comment.get('post_id');\n            const parentId = comment.get('parent_id');\n            const pathsToInvalidate = [\n                postId ? `/api/members/comments/post/${postId}/` : null,\n                parentId ? `/api/members/comments/${parentId}/replies/` : null\n            ].filter(path => path !== null);\n            frame.setHeader('X-Cache-Invalidate', pathsToInvalidate.join(', '));\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async report(frame) {\n        this.#checkMember(frame);\n\n        return await this.service.reportComment(\n            frame.options.id,\n            frame.options?.context?.member\n        );\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get reporters for a specific comment (admin only)\n     * @param {Frame} frame\n     */\n    async getCommentReporters(frame) {\n        const commentId = frame.options.id;\n        return await this.service.getCommentReporters(commentId, {\n Let me look at each file carefully:\n\n### File 1: comments-service.js (getCommentLikes method, lines 260-273)\n\n```javascript\nasync getCommentLikes(commentId, options = {}) {\n    const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId});\n\n    const {page, limit} = options;\n    const result = await this.models.CommentLike.findPage({\n        filter: `comment_id:'${commentId}'`,\n        withRelated: [],\n        order: 'created_at desc',\n        page,\n        limit\n    });\n\n    return result;\n}\n```\n\nIssues I see:\n1. **Line 261**: The method gets the comment but never uses it. It also doesn't check if the comment exists. If the comment doesn't exist, the method will just query likes anyway. This is inconsistent with `getCommentReporters` which also has the same issue. But wait, looking at `getCommentReporters`, it also gets the comment but doesn't use it or check for existence.\n2. **Line 266**: `withRelated: []` is empty - should probably include `member` to get the member details for each like. Looking at `getCommentReporters`, it uses `withRelated: ['member']`. This is likely a bug - we probably want to see who liked the comment.\n\nWait, let me check if there's a functional difference. Looking at the pattern in `getCommentReporters`:\n```javascript\nasync getCommentReporters(commentId, options = {}) {\n    const comment = await this.models.Comment.findOne({id: commentId});\n\n    const {page, limit} = options;\n    const result = await this.models.CommentReport.findPage({\n        filter: `comment_id:'${commentId}'`,\n        withRelated: ['member'],\n        order: 'created_at desc',\n        page,\n        limit\n    });\n\n    return result;\n}\n```\n\nThe `getCommentReporters` method includes `withRelated: ['member']` while `getCommentLikes` has an empty array. This appears to be an oversight - the likes query should probably load the member relation to provide context about who liked the comment, matching the pattern used for reporters.\n\n### File 2: comments-controller.js (getCommentLikes method, lines 369-375)\n\n```javascript\nasync getCommentLikes(frame) {\n    const commentId = frame.data.id;\n    return await this.service.getCommentLikes(commentId, {\n        page: frame.options.page,\n        limit: frame.options.limit\n    });\n}\n```\n\nThere's a mismatch in how the ID is retrieved. The method pulls it from `frame.data.id`, but the validation schema defines `id` under `options`, not `data`. Comparing with `getCommentReporters`, which correctly uses `frame.options.id`, this should follow the same pattern. The current code would pass undefined since the ID isn't in the data object.\n\nLooking at the endpoint configuration in comment-likes.js, the `browse` operation lists `id` as an available option. The endpoint also lacks cache control headers that are typically applied to read-only operations in Ghost's API, which would normally include `Cache-Control` directives for proper caching behavior.\n\nExamining the implementation more closely, I spot a few issues. In comments-service.js at line 266, the `withRelated: []` array is empty when it should include `['member']` to fetch member data for each like. More critically, in comments-controller.js at line 370, the code references `frame.data.id` but the endpoint defines `id` as an option parameter, not a data parameter—this mismatch would cause the method to fail. The endpoint definition itself needs closer inspection to confirm how parameters are actually being passed. pattern in `getCommentReporters` too—it fetches the comment but doesn't reference it afterward. The fetch with `require: true` would throw an error if the comment doesn't exist, so it's functioning as a validation check even though the comment object itself isn't used. This seems intentional for consistency across similar methods, ensuring the endpoint fails early if given an invalid comment ID rather than silently returning an empty list.\n\nThe most pressing issues are the clear bugs: `frame.data.id` needs to be `frame.options.id` in the controller, and `withRelated: []` appears incomplete in another location.\n\nLooking at the endpoint configuration, the `id` parameter is listed in the `options` array, which suggests it should be accessed from `frame.options` rather than `frame.data`. The second issue involves a missing relationship specification that would prevent member details from being included in the API response for comment likes. ters(commentId, {\n        page: frame.options.page,\n        limit: frame.options.limit\n    });\n}\n\nSo the pattern is clear—`getCommentReporters` retrieves the comment ID from `frame.options.id`, meaning `getCommentLikes` should follow the same approach. Line 370 has a bug where it's trying to access `frame.data.id` when it should be `frame.options.id`.\n\nFor the `withRelated` concern, the endpoint needs member details to show who liked the comment. Without including the member relationship, the response would only contain like and comment IDs, stripping away the useful information about which users performed the action.\n\nLooking at `getCommentReporters` as a reference, it explicitly includes `withRelated: ['member']` to display reporter information. HIGH - Missing Member Relation Data** (comments-service.js:266)\n   - Uses `withRelated: []` instead of `withRelated: ['member']`\n   - Without member information, the API response won't show who liked the comment\n   - Makes the endpoint unusable for displaying likers to admins\n\n**3. MEDIUM - Missing Comment Existence Validation** (comments-service.js:260)\n   - Fetches comment but doesn't check if it exists\n   - Should throw a 404 NotFoundError for non-existent comments, not return empty list\n   - Inconsistent with Ghost's error handling patterns (see `getCommentByID`)\n\nThe review has been saved to `/workspace/review.json` with unified diff patches for each defect that can be applied to fix the issues.","stop_reason":null,"session_id":"45558076-0bd2-4f93-aa5f-9abc7de10e07","total_cost_usd":0.10962990000000002,"usage":{"input_tokens":440,"cache_creation_input_tokens":30678,"cache_read_input_tokens":120424,"output_tokens":11760,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":30678,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"modelUsage":{"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001":{"inputTokens":440,"outputTokens":11760,"cacheReadInputTokens":120424,"cacheCreationInputTokens":30678,"webSearchRequests":0,"costUSD":0.10962990000000002,"contextWindow":200000,"maxOutputTokens":32000}},"permission_denials":[],"fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"a185ccba-d8f5-4fd0-8417-f3d471358fe9"}
